ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a smashed egg and butter bun. FOODON_00002473 (egg sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (egg-based composite dish) used as closest parent; no specific code exists for a smashed egg butter bun product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this specific preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one grilled soft bun (about 150 g) filled with smashed egg mayo and topped with whipped salty butter.

nutrient_source.title: Egg mayonnaise sandwich / egg butter bun — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Egg (whole, hard-boiled), Mayonnaise (regular), Butter (salted), Bread roll (enriched white)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Sinpopo Smashed Egg and Salty Butter Bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA. Nutrient values estimated by weighted component assembly: approximately 40 g hard-boiled egg, 20 g mayonnaise, 15 g salted butter, 75 g enriched white bread roll per 150 g serving. Per-100 g values derived from this assembly. USDA FoodData Central FDC IDs used: Egg whole hard-boiled (FDC 173424), Mayonnaise (FDC 172741), Butter salted (FDC 173410), Bread roll enriched (FDC 325871).

gi_source.title: White bread roll GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sinpopo Smashed Egg and Salty Butter Bun. GI estimated at 70 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle being a white bread roll (GI ~70 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international tables). Fat and protein from egg, mayonnaise, and butter will moderately attenuate postprandial glucose response in practice, but the GI is assigned conservatively to the bread component as the primary glycemic driver. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 38.7) / 100 ≈ 27.1, rounded to 27.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~630 mg per serving) from salted butter and mayonnaise
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this item due to its high sodium load from salted butter and mayonnaise; a single serving may contribute over 25% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 70) driven by refined white bread roll, combined with moderate carbohydrate load (~38.7 g per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this item with caution; the refined white bun produces a high glycemic response (GI 70, GL ~27), and the high fat content from butter and mayonnaise may further impair postprandial insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~8.1 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~165 mg per 100 g) from butter, egg yolk, and mayonnaise
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this item due to its high saturated fat and dietary cholesterol content, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels; occasional consumption in small portions is preferable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Smashed Egg and Salty Butter Bun is a Singaporean kopitiam-style snack or light meal popularised by Sinpopo Coffee, a heritage-inspired café chain in Singapore. The dish consists of a handmade soft bread roll that is grilled until lightly golden, then generously filled or topped with a smashed egg mayonnaise mixture and finished with whipped salted butter. The egg mayo component is made from hard-boiled or soft-boiled eggs tossed with mayonnaise to create a creamy, slightly tangy filling, while the salted butter adds a rich, savoury depth. The bun itself is pillowy and slightly crisp on the outside from grilling. The overall flavour profile is rich, creamy, savoury, and mildly eggy, with a contrast of soft interior and lightly toasted crust. Nutritionally, this item is energy-dense at approximately 318 kcal per 100 g, with notable fat content (20.5 g per 100 g, of which 8.1 g is saturated) from butter and mayonnaise, moderate protein (8.2 g per 100 g) from egg, and significant refined carbohydrate (25.8 g per 100 g) from the white bread roll. Sodium is elevated at approximately 420 mg per 100 g due to salted butter and mayonnaise. The glycemic index is estimated at 70 (high), driven by the refined white bun. Clinically, this item warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension (high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high GI and glycemic load), and cardiovascular disease (high saturated fat and cholesterol). It is suitable for vegetarians but not vegans, and contains common allergens including egg, gluten, dairy, and soy.
common_user_queries: What is smashed egg butter bun?
Smashed egg bun GI glycemic index
Can diabetics eat egg butter bun?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Sinpopo Smashed Egg and Salty Butter Bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional database. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted component assembly using USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods and SR Legacy data for individual ingredients (hard-boiled egg, mayonnaise, salted butter, enriched white bread roll). GI was estimated from the white bread roll analogue per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central; no direct laboratory analysis or HPB Foodo entry exists for this specific product. Serving size of 150 g is an estimate based on typical kopitiam bun weight; actual product weight at Sinpopo Coffee may vary. 2. Halal status is listed as false because Sinpopo Coffee - Funan is not listed as a MUIS-certified halal establishment as of the processing date; this should be verified. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and are not directly reported for this specific product; these values carry inherent estimation uncertainty. 4. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white bread roll as the primary glycemic driver; actual GI of the assembled product may be lower due to fat and protein attenuation. 5. The food_group is retained as 'Dairy and eggs' per the input record, reflecting the dominant egg and butter components, though 'Mixed' could also be argued given the bread component.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full multilingual local_names for Singaporean cuisine category (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Estimated per_100g nutrients via USDA FoodData Central component assembly for egg, mayonnaise, salted butter, and white bread roll.
Assigned GI = 70 (high) based on white bread roll analogue from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008.
Computed glycemic_load_per_serving = 27.2 based on 150 g serving and 25.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g.
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are fully consistent.
Documented all estimation assumptions and sentinel value usage in notes.
Set ontology match types to allowed values only (closest_parent, none).
